summary: Persistence Phase 2 testing complete. All 25 backend pytest tests passed (100%). All frontend UI tests passed. MongoDB collections: athletes (25 docs, P2), events (9 docs including TEST_ created, P2). Startup order correctly loads athletes → events → event_notes → recommendations → recomputes derived data. daysSinceActivity and daysAway are recomputed from stored timestamps on load. Event notes correctly merged into events (Winter Showcase shows 9 notes captured). Admin status shows Phase 2 with 10 persisted collections and 2 in-memory-only.
seed_data_creation: TEST_Phase2_Event created during pytest (POST /api/events). TEST_PHASE2_NOTE created during pytest (POST /api/events/event_1/notes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Persistence Phase 2 fully tested. Key architecture: on startup, seed_if_empty seeds athletes and events if collections empty, then loads from DB into memory, then recomputes all derived data (interventions, alerts, signals, snapshot). daysSinceActivity (athletes) and daysAway (events) are recomputed from stored timestamps. Events stored WITHOUT capturedNotes (those in event_notes collection, merged at load time). Athletes are read-only (no write paths). Events have write paths (POST new event, PATCH checklist). MongoDB collections: athletes (25), events (7+ seeded), event_notes, recommendations. Admin status shows Phase 2 with startup_order field.

athletes_persistence: Athletes have correct fields: id, fullName, gradYear, position, recruitingStage, daysSinceActivity, lastActivity, etc.
GET /api/athletes/{id} returns single athlete from DB
daysSinceActivity is recomputed from stored lastActivity timestamp
Athlete IDs follow pattern athlete_1 through athlete_25
events_persistence: GET /api/events returns events grouped as past/upcoming
Events have daysAway recomputed from stored date
GET /api/events/{id} returns event without capturedNotes field
Winter Showcase (event_0) correctly marked as past with negative daysAway
POST /api/events creates new event and persists to MongoDB
PATCH /api/events/{event_id}/checklist/{item_id} toggles and persists to MongoDB
event_notes_merged: Events in list have capturedNotes merged (Winter Showcase shows 9 notes)
integration: Mission Control returns data with persisted athletes + events
Program Intelligence returns 25 athletes across 3 grad years
admin_status: Admin status shows 10 persisted MongoDB collections
Admin status shows 2 in-memory-only collections (schools, interventions)
support_pod: Support Pod works for multiple athletes (athlete_5, athlete_10, athlete_20)
seed_if_empty: Events count at least 7 (7 seeded + test-created)

page_loads: Mission Control: 4 priority alerts, 12 athletes monitoring, live feed
Events: Upcoming (8), Past (1) - Winter Showcase shows 9 notes captured
Program Intelligence: 25 athletes, 9 events, 23 recommendations shown in header
Admin Status: Phase 2 badge, 10 persisted collections displayed with counts and phase badges
admin_status_page: Athletes collection shows 25 count with P2 badge
Events collection shows 9 count with P2 badge
In-memory only section shows schools (10) and interventions (18)

verified_persistence_features: MongoDB seed-if-empty: athletes (25) seeded on first run, skipped on subsequent runs
MongoDB seed-if-empty: events (7) seeded on first run, skipped on subsequent runs
GET /api/athletes: Returns 25 athletes loaded from MongoDB
GET /api/athletes/{id}: Returns single athlete from DB with all fields
daysSinceActivity recomputed from stored lastActivity timestamp on every load
GET /api/events: Returns events grouped as past/upcoming with daysAway recomputed
GET /api/events/{id}: Returns single event without capturedNotes (those in event_notes)
POST /api/events: Creates new event, persists to MongoDB with computed daysAway/status
PATCH /api/events/{event_id}/checklist/{item_id}: Toggles checklist item, persists to MongoDB
Event notes from event_notes collection correctly merged into events during load
GET /api/events/{event_id}/notes: Returns notes from MongoDB (Phase 1 persistence)
POST /api/events/{event_id}/notes: Creates notes in MongoDB (Phase 1 persistence)
Startup order: athletes → events → event_notes → recommendations → recompute derived data
GET /api/admin/status: Shows Phase 2, 10 persisted collections, 2 in-memory-only, startup_order
